future_activation_order: validate entry assembly, kernel stack, GDT selectors, IDT containment, and user-return contract
write canonical LSTAR and CSTAR targets plus reviewed STAR selectors and SFMASK
initialize per-CPU kernel GS base and user FS/GS ownership
initialize unique per-CPU TSC_AUX after CPU topology identity is stable
read back and independently validate every programmed field
set EFER.SCE last and publish syscall authority only after the complete transaction passes

machine_check_policy.mcg_cap_allowed_bits: BANK_CNT[7:0], CTLP[8], and qemu64 compatibility bit[24]
machine_check_policy.qemu64_bit24_policy: opaque emulator compatibility observation; no AMD architectural meaning or authority claimed
machine_check_policy.mcg_ctl_rule: when CTLP is set, the frozen qemu64 profile must read all ones; this value is not generalized to hardware
machine_check_policy.future_handler_rule: snapshot valid bank status and status-qualified address/misc data into retained storage before any clear; fail closed on PCC, lost overflow evidence, recursion, or incoherent per-CPU state

performance_monitoring_policy.unsupported_policy: no event selector, counter, global control, overflow status, or RDPMC access

source_register.title: AMD64 Architecture Programmer's Manual Volume 2: System Programming
source_register.use: system-linkage, FS/GS/TSC_AUX, global MCA, bank sequencing, and performance-monitoring MSR semantics

source_register.title: QEMU x86 CPU model configuration
source_register.use: qemu64 compatibility-profile and non-production boundary; runtime CPUID remains the executable support gate

non_claims: PKMSR1 is a single-BSP qemu64 development observation and does not qualify the AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D target or any physical machine.
Observed zero system-linkage values are not architectural reset guarantees; AMD documents several reset values as undefined.
No STAR, LSTAR, CSTAR, SFMASK, EFER.SCE, FS/GS base, TSC_AUX, MCA, PMU, control-register, or other privileged write occurs; TSC_AUX is also not read because RDTSCP is absent.
No SYSCALL, SYSRET, SWAPGS, RDTSCP, RDPMC, machine-check delivery, MCA bank walk, error clear, PMU event programming, interrupt enable, or ring transition is executed.
The qemu64-only MCG_CAP bit 24 and all-ones MCG_CTL observations are compatibility-profile facts, not AMD architectural semantics or target-hardware policy.
Global MCA inventory does not qualify product-specific bank meanings, retained crash recovery, recursive fault containment, or a machine-check handler.
The absent qemu64 PMU path proves only fail-closed unsupported behavior; it does not qualify Intel architectural PMU or AMD PerfMonV2 behavior.
No AP-local ownership, scheduler integration, user ABI, target firmware, second host, key, signature, capability, authority, physical-media effect, release, or production promotion is claimed.
